findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 32. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: The PDF uses a compact horizontal table layout fitting all columns on one page, while the HTML renders a much more vertically expanded layout with narrower columns, causing each row to wrap extensively and the table to span many more vertical pages.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a single-page compact table with all 18 cases visible. HTML stretches the same content vertically with each row taking significantly more vertical space due to text wrapping in narrow columns.

findings.description: Table header labels are slightly abbreviated in the HTML version compared to the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF header: 'Owner - Facility' → HTML: 'Owner - Facility'; PDF: 'As Built Flow MGD' → HTML: 'As Built Flow MGD'; PDF: 'Total Cases in Previous 12 Months' → HTML: 'Total Cases in Previous 12 Months'. These match, but column 'Case Number' is split across lines differently.
